---
title: "Blog"
subtitle: "Guides and posts by roadmap step"
description: "First Break AI cohort blog — step-by-step guides that follow the roadmap. Run models locally, understand inference, and build in the open."
css: ../styles/blog.css
---

Cohort guides and posts are organized by the [Roadmap](../roadmap.qmd). Start with the step you're on.

## [Step 1]{.section-tag .step-1} First use of AI for coding {.section-heading}

::: {.section-blurb}
Set up a Quarto blog, GitHub, and your AI IDE. Posts for this step are still in progress — follow the roadmap for the current task list.
:::

::: {.blog-cards}

:::: {.blog-card .blog-card-step-1 .blog-card-no-post}
[Step 1]{.blog-card-label}

### First use of AI for coding

::: {.blog-card-desc}
Set up a Quarto blog, GitHub, and use your AI IDE.
:::

[See Roadmap](../roadmap.qmd){.blog-card-link}
::::

:::

## [Step 2]{.section-tag .step-2} Run a model locally {.section-heading}

::: {.section-blurb}
Run Qwen3 0.6B on your laptop and trace every operation from tokenization to token output — first in pure C, then through the HuggingFace `transformers` API.
:::

::: {.blog-cards}

:::: {.blog-card .blog-card-step-2}
[Step 2]{.blog-card-label}

### Run Qwen3 0.6B in pure C

::: {.blog-card-desc}
Run Qwen3 0.6B in pure C. Tokens, BPE, chat templates, attention, KV cache — all in one C binary.
:::

[Read guide](qwen3-run-locally.qmd){.blog-card-link}
::::

:::: {.blog-card .blog-card-step-2}
[Step 2]{.blog-card-label}

### GGUF vs SafeTensors

::: {.blog-card-desc}
Model weight formats compared. GGUF, SafeTensors, pickle security, quantization, mmap, and why we start with pure C.
:::

[Read guide](model-formats-gguf-safetensors.qmd){.blog-card-link}
::::

:::

## [Step 3]{.section-tag .step-3} Inference deep dive {.section-heading}

::: {.section-blurb}
Beyond running a model — inference engines, batching, quantization formats, and serving. Posts in progress.
:::

::: {.blog-cards}

:::: {.blog-card .blog-card-step-3 .blog-card-no-post}
[Step 3]{.blog-card-label}

### Inference deep dive

::: {.blog-card-desc}
vLLM, quantization, batching, APIs.
:::

[See Roadmap](../roadmap.qmd){.blog-card-link}
::::

:::

## [Step 4]{.section-tag .step-4} Training fundamentals {.section-heading}

::: {.section-blurb}
Read training curves like an engineer, then train your own. Distributed training, loss spikes, and what real LLM W&B dashboards actually show you.
:::

::: {.blog-cards}

:::: {.blog-card .blog-card-step-4}
[Step 4]{.blog-card-label}

### Reading the Curves (LLM training)

::: {.blog-card-desc}
W&B checklists, tokens vs steps, OLMo checkpoints, Marin 32B and QK-Norm warm-start—how real LLMs learn, spike, and stabilize.
:::

[Read guide](reading-the-curves-llm-training.qmd){.blog-card-link}
::::

:::

::: {.blog-back}
[← Back to Roadmap](../roadmap.qmd)
:::
